questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (3). 👉 Replace "yesterday at the Rajpath" with "at the Rajpath yesterday" 📌 Error concept: The error involves the incorrect sequencing of the Adverb of Time and the Adverb of Place. Grammatical concept: In English grammar, when multiple adverbs follow a verb, they generally follow the M-P-T rule (Manner, Place, Time). 1. Manner (How): professionally 2. Place (Where): at the Rajpath 3. Time (When): yesterday In the given sentence, the "Time" (yesterday) was placed before the "Place" (at the Rajpath). According to the standard examiner-oriented rule, the Place must precede the Time.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The opposition leader decided to ________ the government's inconsistent policies during the televised debate, pointing out the stark contradictions in the official reports.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - Call out means to publicly criticize someone or point out their faults, which fits the context of an opposition leader pointing out "stark contradictions" in government policies. Meanings and Examples of Options: Call back: वापस फोन करना / वापस बुलाना - to telephone someone who has telephoned you, or to ask someone to return. Example: I am busy right now; I will call you back in an hour. Call off: रद्द करना / वापस लेना - To cancel something that was planned, especially an event or an agreement. Call up: फोन करना / सेना में भर्ती के लिए बुलाना - To telephone someone, or to summon someone for military service. Example: Many young men were called up to fight during the war. Call out: आलोचना करना / पोल खोलना - To criticize someone publicly for their behavior or to draw attention to a mistake. Example: It is important to call out injustice whenever you see it.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The defense attorney argued that the evidence presented by the prosecution was not pertinent for the case and should therefore be dismissed immediately.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The underlined part "pertinent for" is incorrect because the word pertinent is an adjective that takes a fixed preposition. In English grammar, when you want to express that something is relevant or applicable to a particular matter, you must use pertinent to. Pertinent to - उचित / प्रासंगिक होना - Relevant or applicable to a particular matter; apposite. Example: During the interview, please only share details that are pertinent to your previous work experience.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: The manager, along with his entire team of developers, were invited to the conference in Bangalore.
Me and my friends are planning a trip to the beach next weekend.
She did not believe him, but he told the truth anyway.
It was her who organized the entire event without any external assistance.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct. The subject "She" is followed by the correct negative past tense "did not believe," and the second clause "he told the truth" Correctly uses the simple past tense to show a completed action. Detailed Analysis of Other wrong Options: Option A: Error: The verb should be "was" instead of "were." When subjects are joined by phrases like "along with," "as well as," or "together with," the verb must agree with the first subject (The manager), which is singular. Option B: Error: It should be "My friends and I." When used as a subject, the personal pronoun must be in the nominative case (I), not the objective case (Me). Also, politeness rules dictate that "others" come before "I." Option D: Error: It should be "It was she." When a pronoun follows a form of the "to be" verb (is, was, were) and acts as a subject complement, it must be in the subjective case (she/he/I).

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. After the surgery, the patient began to deteriorate rapidly and was discharged from the hospital in high spirits.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The sentence contains a contextual contradiction. It mentions that the patient was "discharged in high spirits" (happy/healthy), which contradicts the word "deteriorate" (to get worse). Therefore, we need a word that means "to recover" or "to get better." Recuperate means to recover from illness or exertion. Grammatical concept: This is a Sentence Improvement question based on Contextual Logic. In SSC exams, the examiner often provides a "clue phrase" (like in high spirits) at the end of the sentence to indicate whether the underlined part should be positive or negative. Since being discharged in high spirits is positive, the underlined verb must also be positive. Meanings and Examples of Options: Relapse: दोबारा बीमार पड़ना -To suffer a deterioration after a period of improvement. Example: After a week of being healthy, the patient suffered a relapse. Recuperate: स्वस्थ हो जाना / चंगा होना - To recover from illness or exertion. Example: She spent a month in the mountains to recuperate after her surgery. Stagnate: स्थिर हो जाना / प्रगति रुक जाना - To cease to flow or move; to become inactive or dull. Example: Without new investments, the local economy will stagnate.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. In a world dominated by rapid technological shifts, companies that remain ________ and refuse to modernize their infrastructure often find themselves obsolete within a decade.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - Intransigent means refusing to change one's views or to agree about something. In the context of the sentence, companies that "refuse to modernize" are being stubborn or inflexible, which leads them to become "obsolete." Meanings and Examples of Options: Intransigent- हठी / कट्टर / समझौताहीन - Unwilling or refusing to change one's views or to agree about something. Example: Despite the evidence, the committee remained intransigent in their opposition to the new policy. Versatile- बहुमुखी / परिवर्तनशील - Able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions or activities. Example: Leather is a versatile material that can be used for clothing, shoes, and furniture. Malleable- लचीला / जिसे ढाला जा सके - Easily influenced; pliable; (of a metal) able to be hammered or pressed out of shape. Example: Knowledgeable teachers can shape the malleable minds of young students Overt- प्रत्यक्ष / खुला - Done or shown openly; plainly apparent; not secret or hidden Example: There was no overt hostility between the two rivals, but the tension was visible.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: The newly elected state government, in its ambitious bid to transform the rural landscape,
has not only constructed a high-tech secondary school with modern amenities
but also a multispecialty hospital for the poor residing in the remote districts.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (2). 👉 Replace "has not only constructed" with "has constructed not only" 📌 Error concept: This is a classic case of Faulty Parallelism involving the correlative conjunction "not only... but also." Grammatical concept: The rule for "not only... but also" is that both parts of the pair must be followed by the same part of speech (Noun with Noun, Verb with Verb, etc.). Let's look at the structure of the original sentence: Part 2: ...not only [constructed (Verb) + a high-tech secondary school (Noun Phrase)] Part 3: ...but also [a multispecialty hospital (Noun Phrase)] Corrected version: has constructed [not only a high-tech secondary school] [but also a multispecialty hospital].

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: They decided to object to the new regulations during the meeting.
options: The shiny object in the distance caught the traveler’s eye.
Many residents object to the construction of the highway.
I must object if you intend to skip the safety protocols.
Did anyone object to the proposal during the final vote?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 👉 Explanation - This is a "Homonym" question. Homonyms are words that have the same spelling and pronunciation but different meanings. In the Question: "Object" is used as a Verb which means to express disapproval, opposition, or a difference of opinion (आपत्ति करना / विरोध करना). In Option A: "Object" is used as a Noun which means a material thing that can be seen and touched (वस्तु / चीज़). This is a different grammatical category and meaning, making it the correct homonym choice. WHY OTHER OPTIONS ARE WRONG: All other options (B, C, and D) use OBJECT as a Verb in the sense of protesting or disagreeing, preserving the same core meaning as the highlighted word in the main sentence.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. (A) This includes understanding how to manage credit cards and avoid high-interest debt traps. (B) Therefore, integrating these concepts into the school curriculum is a necessary step for long-term economic stability. (C) Financial literacy is the ability to understand and effectively use various financial skills. (D) Without these fundamental skills, young adults often struggle with basic tasks like tax filing or retirement planning.
questions.explanation: 👉 The Correct Option is (A) – CADB. 📌 Explanation - Step-by-Step Logic: The Opening (Sentence C): This sentence introduces and defines the main topic ("Financial literacy"). It is an independent statement that provides the foundation for the rest of the passage. The Elaboration (Sentence A): This starts with "This includes...", which serves as a pointer to the "various financial skills" mentioned in Sentence C. It provides specific examples (credit cards and debt). [Link: C-A] The Consequence (Sentence D): This sentence explains what happens "Without these fundamental skills" (referring back to the skills in 3 and the examples in A). It highlights the struggle of young adults. [Link: A-D] The Conclusion (Sentence B): It begins with the transition word "Therefore," offering a solution (school curriculum) to the problems mentioned in the previous sentences. [Link: D-B] FINAL PARAGRAPH: Financial literacy is the ability to understand and effectively use various financial skills. This includes understanding how to manage credit cards and avoid high-interest debt traps. Without these fundamental skills, young adults often struggle with basic tasks like tax filing or retirement planning. Therefore, integrating these concepts into the school curriculum is a necessary step for long-term economic stability.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The professor could have been explaining differential topology for hours.
options: Differential topology could have be being explained for hours by the professor.
Differential topology could have explained for hours by the professor.
Differential topology could have been being explained for hours by the professor.
Differential topology could have had been been explained for hours by the professor.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This question is based on Active/Passive Voice for the Modal Perfect Continuous tense (could have been explaining). To convert this into passive voice: The Object (Differential topology) becomes the Subject. The Modal structure "could have been" remains. The "-ing" form (explaining) is replaced by "being + V3" (being explained). The Subject (The professor) becomes the Object after "by". Grammatical concept: In SSC exams, converting Perfect Continuous tenses (especially with Modals) into passive is a frequent trap. While many traditional grammarians avoid this structure for being wordy, for competitive exams like CGL, you must follow this structure for voice. Active Formula: Subject + Modal + have + been + V{ing} + Object Passive Formula: Object + Modal + have + been + being + V3 + by + Subject.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The lawyer said that had the evidence not been tampered with, the verdict could have been different.
options: "If the evidence had not tampered with, the verdict could change," said the lawyer.
"If the evidence were not tampered with, the verdict could have changed," the lawyer said.
"Had the evidence not been tampered with, the verdict could have been different," said the lawyer.
"Had not the evidence tampered, the verdict may differ," said he.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - This is a Direct/Indirect Speech question involving a Conditional Sentence (Type 3). The original sentence is in Indirect Speech and uses an inverted conditional structure (had the evidence not been...). Grammatical concept: This sentence deals with Conditionals in Reported Speech. Type 3 Conditional: Used for past situations that did not happen. Structure: If + Subject + had + V3 ... Subject + could/would + have + V3 Inversion Rule: You can replace "If" by starting with "Had": Had + Subject + V3... Tense Consistency: In Indirect speech, if the reporting verb is in the past (said), the conditional type 3 remains in the same past perfect form because it is already at the "furthest" past point.

questions.question: The EU’s top global warming advisory body, the European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change (ESABCC), has called on the bloc’s members to find ways to deal ___(1)___ rising temperatures. The agency’s advice to prepare for a temperature rise of about 2.8 degrees Celsius by 2100 ___(2)___ particularly sobering. For decades, Europe’s climate policy has focused on emissions reduction commitments. The advisory, issued on Tuesday, acknowledges that mitigation must be accompanied by measures to build ___(3)___ against the worst effects of a warming world. It is also a ___(4)____ recognition that the Paris Pact’s ambition of limiting global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels is increasingly out of reach. Most studies identify Europe as the fastest-warming continent. Floods, lethal heatwaves and ___(5)____ forest fires almost every year of this decade are warnings that EU policymakers have to find ways to save lives and protect livelihoods. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) with. 💢 Explanation - The verb "deal" is a phrasal verb that takes the fixed preposition "with" when it means to take action in order to solve a problem or handle a situation. Why other options are wrong: "Deal about," "Deal in" (means to trade), and "Deal out" (means to distribute) do not fit the context of managing rising temperatures.

questions.question: The EU’s top global warming advisory body, the European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change (ESABCC), has called on the bloc’s members to find ways to deal ___(1)___ rising temperatures. The agency’s advice to prepare for a temperature rise of about 2.8 degrees Celsius by 2100 ___(2)___ particularly sobering. For decades, Europe’s climate policy has focused on emissions reduction commitments. The advisory, issued on Tuesday, acknowledges that mitigation must be accompanied by measures to build ___(3)___ against the worst effects of a warming world. It is also a ___(4)____ recognition that the Paris Pact’s ambition of limiting global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels is increasingly out of reach. Most studies identify Europe as the fastest-warming continent. Floods, lethal heatwaves and ___(5)____ forest fires almost every year of this decade are warnings that EU policymakers have to find ways to save lives and protect livelihoods. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) - is. 💢 Explanation - The subject of the sentence is "advice," which is an uncountable noun and always takes a singular verb. Since the passage discusses a current state/report, the simple present "is" is the most appropriate. Why other options are wrong: "Are" is plural; "was" indicates the past (but the advice is still relevant); "has" would require a participle (e.g., has been).

questions.question: The EU’s top global warming advisory body, the European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change (ESABCC), has called on the bloc’s members to find ways to deal ___(1)___ rising temperatures. The agency’s advice to prepare for a temperature rise of about 2.8 degrees Celsius by 2100 ___(2)___ particularly sobering. For decades, Europe’s climate policy has focused on emissions reduction commitments. The advisory, issued on Tuesday, acknowledges that mitigation must be accompanied by measures to build ___(3)___ against the worst effects of a warming world. It is also a ___(4)____ recognition that the Paris Pact’s ambition of limiting global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels is increasingly out of reach. Most studies identify Europe as the fastest-warming continent. Floods, lethal heatwaves and ___(5)____ forest fires almost every year of this decade are warnings that EU policymakers have to find ways to save lives and protect livelihoods. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) resilience. 💢 Explanation - After the verb "build," we require a Noun to act as the object. "Resilience" (the capacity to recover quickly from difficulties) is a noun. Why other options are wrong: "Resilient" is an adjective; "resiliently" is an adverb.

questions.question: The EU’s top global warming advisory body, the European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change (ESABCC), has called on the bloc’s members to find ways to deal ___(1)___ rising temperatures. The agency’s advice to prepare for a temperature rise of about 2.8 degrees Celsius by 2100 ___(2)___ particularly sobering. For decades, Europe’s climate policy has focused on emissions reduction commitments. The advisory, issued on Tuesday, acknowledges that mitigation must be accompanied by measures to build ___(3)___ against the worst effects of a warming world. It is also a ___(4)____ recognition that the Paris Pact’s ambition of limiting global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels is increasingly out of reach. Most studies identify Europe as the fastest-warming continent. Floods, lethal heatwaves and ___(5)____ forest fires almost every year of this decade are warnings that EU policymakers have to find ways to save lives and protect livelihoods. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) tacit. 💢 Explanation - Tacit means understood or implied without being stated directly. The passage suggests the recognition is "increasingly" being felt as goals become "out of reach," making it an unspoken or secondary admission. Tacit - मौन / अंतर्निहित - Understood or implied without being stated. Example - There was a tacit agreement between the two rivals to keep the peace. Why other options are wrong: "Explicit" (clear) is the opposite; "booming" refers to sound or economy; "stated" is redundant with "recognition."

questions.question: The EU’s top global warming advisory body, the European Scientific Advisory Board on Climate Change (ESABCC), has called on the bloc’s members to find ways to deal ___(1)___ rising temperatures. The agency’s advice to prepare for a temperature rise of about 2.8 degrees Celsius by 2100 ___(2)___ particularly sobering. For decades, Europe’s climate policy has focused on emissions reduction commitments. The advisory, issued on Tuesday, acknowledges that mitigation must be accompanied by measures to build ___(3)___ against the worst effects of a warming world. It is also a ___(4)____ recognition that the Paris Pact’s ambition of limiting global warming to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels is increasingly out of reach. Most studies identify Europe as the fastest-warming continent. Floods, lethal heatwaves and ___(5)____ forest fires almost every year of this decade are warnings that EU policymakers have to find ways to save lives and protect livelihoods. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) devastating. 💢 Explanation - We need a Present Participle (Adjective) to describe the "forest fires." Devastating means causing great destruction. Devastating - विनाशकारी - Highly destructive or damaging. Example - The hurricane had a devastating effect on the coastal village. Why other options are wrong: "Constructing" and "restored" are positive/neutral terms that don't fit "forest fires"; "devasted" is the wrong form (should be devastating to describe the nature of the fire).

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Coulometer. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Coulometer - कूलोमीटर - an apparatus for determining the quantity of electricity passed through an electrolytic cell Example: The coulometer measured the amount of electricity used in the experiment. Hierarchy - श्रेणी व्यवस्था - a system of organization in which people or things are ranked according to their importance or status Example: The company's hierarchy was clearly defined, with the CEO at the top. Quinquagenarian - पचास वर्षीय - a person who is fifty years old Example: The quinquagenarian celebrated his 50th birthday with a big party. Olfactophobia - घ्राण भय - a fear or aversion to smells Example: Her olfactophobia made it difficult for her to be around strong perfumes.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The bill for dinner was exorbitant as per the set standards by government.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Outrageous. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Exorbitant - अत्यधिक - excessively high or unreasonable Example: The bill for dinner was exorbitant. Outrageous - अत्यधिक - unreasonably high or shocking Example: The prices of the designer handbags were outrageous. Forge - बनाना/घड़ना - to create or shape something Example: The blacksmith will forge the metal into a sword. Modest - मध्यम - reasonably priced or humble Example: The hotel room rates were modest. Outlandish - विचित्र - unconventional or bizarre Example: The outlandish design of the building made it a tourist attraction.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Disinter
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Inter. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Disinter - दफनाए गए को निकालना - to dig up or uncover something buried Example: The court ordered to disinter the remains. Venting - वेंटिंग - expressing or giving release to an emotion Example: She was venting her frustration on social media. Doltish - मूर्खतापूर्ण - foolish or stupid Example: His doltish behaviour was embarrassing. Inter - दफनाना - to bury a dead body Example: The family decided to inter the deceased in the village cemetery. Hefty - भारी - large or heavy Example: The hefty box was difficult to lift.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The news about the celebrity's death was a ______, and he appeared on TV to clarify.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Hoax. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Arduous - कठिन - requiring a lot of effort or energy, extremely tiring Example: The hikers found the climb to the summit arduous due to the steep terrain. Hoax - झूठी खबर - a false or misleading statement or situation intended to deceive or trick people Example: The viral message claiming to be from a reputable bank was a hoax designed to steal personal information. Massive - विशाल - extremely large or impressive in size, amount, or degree Example: The concert was a massive success, attracting thousands of fans from around the world. Mellow - शांत - softened or tempered, often in a pleasant or soothing way, relaxed and calm Example: The mellow sounds of jazz music created a peaceful atmosphere at the café.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Yellow press
options: Journalism that is sensational or biased, often prioritizing attracting readers over accuracy or fairness.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Journalism that is sensational or biased, often prioritizing attracting readers over accuracy or fairness. Let see the meaning of the idiom: Yellow Press - सनसनीखेज पत्रकारिता Example: The yellow press was criticized for spreading unverified rumours about the celebrity.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Tardy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Sluggish. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Tardy - देर से आने वाला - slow or late in arriving or happening Example: The employee's tardy arrival disrupted the meeting. Punctual - समयनिष्ठ - arriving or doing something on time Example: She is a punctual person and always arrives on schedule. Sluggish - धीमा - slow or inactive, often in a way that is annoying or unnatural Example: The sluggish pace of the internet was frustrating. Drivel - बकवास - nonsense or foolish talk Example: The movie was a complete drivel and a waste of time. Merge - विलय - to combine or join two or more things Example: The company will merge with its competitor next quarter.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The adroit use of technology helped the team complete the project efficiently.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Inept. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Adroit - चतुर - skillful or clever in a quick and adaptable way Example: The adroit use of technology helped the team complete the project efficiently. Inept - अयोग्य - lacking skill or competence Example: The inept handling of the situation made things worse. Heroic - वीरतापूर्ण - showing great courage or bravery Example: The heroic actions of the firefighters saved many lives. Frugal - थड़ा खर्चीला या किफायती careful and prudent in using resources Example: She was frugal with her expenses and managed to save money. Frisk - छानबीन करना - to search or examine someone or something Example: The security personnel will frisk you before entering the building.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A strong feeling of opposition, anger or hatred
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Animosity. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Terse - संक्षिप्त - brief and to the point, often in a rude way Example: His terse reply showed his displeasure. Amusia - संगीत की समझ न होना - a condition where a person lacks musical ability or understanding Example: She was diagnosed with amusia and couldn't recognize melodies. Modus - तरीका - a way or method of doing something Example: The modus operandi of the team was to work collaboratively. Animosity - द्वेष - a strong feeling of opposition, anger, or hatred Example: The animosity between the two rival teams was palpable.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The act of ending something
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Termination. Let’s see the meaning of all words: Termination - समाप्ति - the act of ending something Example: The termination of the contract was unexpected. Stroller - बेबी कार - a chair with wheels for carrying a baby Example: She pushed the baby in a stroller. Nerd - बौद्धिक व्यक्ति - a person who is very interested in technical or intellectual subjects Example: He's a bit of a nerd when it comes to computers. Valise - छोटा सूटकेस - a small suitcase Example: She packed her clothes in a valise.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Vedanga, or the limbs of the Veda?
questions.explanation: The Vedangas are exactly six auxiliary disciplines (limbs) necessary for the study of the Vedas: 1.Shiksha: Phonetics and pronunciation. 2.Kalpa: Ritual instructions and procedures. 3.Vyakarana: Grammar and linguistic analysis. 4.Nirukta: Etymology and explanation of words. 5.Chandas: Vedic meter and prosody. 6.Jyotisha: Astronomy and astrology for timing rituals.

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct options: Assertion (A): The Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam recognizes electronic records as primary evidence. Reason (R): Section 62 states that electronic records are secondary unless accompanied by a printed certificate.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the collect explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, R is false
A is false, R is true.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true: Under the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA), 2023, electronic or digital records are explicitly recognized as primary evidence. Section 57 of the BSA simplifies the process by deeming original electronic records from proper custody as primary evidence, granting them the same legal effect and validity as traditional paper documents. Reason (R) is false: Section 62 of the BSA does not state that electronic records are secondary by default. Instead, Section 62 outlines the general process for proving the contents of electronic records. While Section 63 requires a certificate for the admissibility of secondary electronic evidence (like printouts or copies), the law specifically allows for the original electronic record to be admitted as primary evidence without such a certificate if produced directly in court.

questions.question: When was the National Pension System launched in India?
questions.explanation: The National Pension System (NPS) was launched by the Government of India on 1 January 2004. Initially, the scheme was mandatory only for new Central Government recruits (except for the armed forces) who joined service on or after that date. The scheme has evolved through several key phases: -1 January 2004: Mandatory launch for new Central Government employees. -2004 onwards: Adopted by most State Governments for their new employees at various dates. -1 May 2009: Extended to all citizens of India, including workers in the unorganised sector, on a voluntary basis. -December 2011: The Corporate NPS model was introduced to allow private sector companies to offer NPS to their employees. -18 September 2024: Launch of NPS Vatsalya, a long-term savings schemes specifically for minors

questions.question: Which of the following actions is NOT allowed for a player in Handball?
options: Pulling or punching the ball out of an opponent’s hands
Blocking the ball with the torso above the knees
Dribbling the ball before making a pass
Intercepting the ball mid-air using both hands
questions.explanation: In handball, defensive players are prohibited from forcibly removing the ball from an opponent's possession. According to the International Handball Federation (IHF) rules, it is illegal to: -Pull or snatch the ball with one or both hands. -Punch, hit, or strike the ball out of an opponent's grasp. These actions are considered fouls to ensure fair play and player safety, often resulting in penalties such as a free throw or a yellow card.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book ‘Why I am a Hindu’?
questions.explanation: Why I Am a Hindu is a 2018 book by Indian politician Shashi Tharoor. In the book, Tharoor writes about the history of Hinduism and its core tenets, as well as socio-cultural developments in India that relate to the religion, while elucidating his own religious convictions

questions.question: Consider the statements about Dumhal, Which is/are correct? 1.It celebrates apple harvests in southern Kashmir. 2.Exclusively wattal men plant a banner before dancing.
questions.explanation: Statement 2 is correct: Dumhal is a traditional dance performed exclusively by men of the Wattal community/tribe in Jammu & Kashmir. A key ritual of this dance is that the performers place a banner or flag into the ground before beginning the dance, which they then perform around. Statement 1 is incorrect: While Dumhal is a ceremonial folk dance from Kashmir, it is generally performed on special, auspicious occasions, or on special set locations/locations, rather than being specifically defined as a celebration of apple harvests.

questions.question: Which of the following exercise is not correctly matched with country name?
questions.explanation: Exercise and Country -Hand-in-Hand — India and China -Surya Kiran — India and Nepal -Sampriti — India and Bangladesh -Nomadic Elephant — India and Mongolia -Garuda Shakti — India and Indonesia -Mitra Shakti — India and Sri Lanka -Dharma Guardian — India and Japan -Maitree — India and Thailand -Ekuverin — India and Maldives -Khanjar — India and Kyrgyzstan -Lamitiye — India and Seychelles -Indra — India and Russia -Konkan — India and United Kingdom -Varuna — India and France

questions.question: What is the main goal of the ‘Green Credit Programme’ launched in 2024?
questions.explanation: The Green Credit Programme (GCP) is a market-based initiative launched by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change to incentivize voluntary environmental actions. Its main goal is to reward individuals, communities, and industries for activities that positively impact the environment, specifically focusing on eco-restoration and environmental conservation. Key features of the programme include: •Market-Based Incentives: Participants earn "Green Credits" for activities like tree plantation, water conservation, and waste management. •Tradable Credits: These credits can be traded on a domestic market platform, providing financial benefits for sustainable practices. •Alignment with Global Goals: The program supports India's Mission LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) and global climate commitments like the Paris Agreement. •Focus on Restoration: In 2024, the government updated guidelines to emphasize ecosystem restoration over simple tree counting, requiring at least five years of restoration work and a 40% canopy density to earn credits.

questions.question: In which state Rajesh Kumar has been appointed as Chief Secretary?
questions.explanation: Rajesh Kumar (specifically Rajesh Kumar Meena, a 1988-batch IAS officer) was appointed as the Chief Secretary of Maharashtra, taking charge around June 30, 2025, to succeed Sujata Saunik. He served in this role before being succeeded by Rajesh Aggarwal in late 2025.

questions.question: Consider the statements about Gaganyaan mission, which is/are correct? 1.The crew capacity is 3. 2.It is planned to orbit Earth at approximately 500km altitude.
questions.explanation: 1. The crew capacity is 3: Correct. The Gaganyaan crew module is designed to carry a crew of 3 members to Low Earth Orbit (LEO). 2. It is planned to orbit Earth at approximately 500km altitude: Incorrect. The planned orbital altitude for the mission is approximately 400 km

questions.question: Match the Column:- Branch of Study Study About A) Serpentology 1) Study of viruses B) Taxonomy 2) Study of classification of organisms C) Physiology 3) Study of snakes D) Virology 4) Study of function at cell, tissue and organ system level
questions.explanation: Branch of Study Study About A) Serpentology 3) Study of snakes B) Taxonomy 2) Study of classification of organisms C) Physiology 4) Study of function at cell, tissue and organ system level D) Virology 1) Study of viruses

questions.question: Fill in the blanks: The Union Home Minister chairs a Zonal Council Meeting as _____Chairman.
questions.explanation: The Union Home Minister chairs a Zonal Council Meeting as ex-officio Chairman. Established under the States Re-organisation Act of 1956, the Union Home Minister acts as the chairman for all five Zonal Councils in India to promote interstate cooperation.

questions.question: Which of the following is known as the Paper tax in India? 1.Goods and Services tax 2.Wealth tax
questions.explanation: Wealth tax is commonly referred to as a "paper tax" in India because it historically yielded very low revenue despite having high administrative and compliance costs. It was considered a tax that existed more "on paper" than as an effective revenue-generating tool. Other taxes like Gift tax and Estate Duty have also been categorized this way for similar reasons. In India, the Wealth Tax Act of 1957 was eventually abolished in 2015 and replaced by an additional surcharge on high-income earners. Goods and Services tax (GST) is a comprehensive indirect tax implemented in 2017 to replace multiple other indirect taxes (like Excise Duty and VAT). Unlike "paper taxes," GST is a major revenue stream for the Indian government and is a transaction-based consumption tax rather than a tax on accumulated assets

questions.question: Which BRICS country has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Among the BRICS nations, Russia has the longest coastline by a significant margin. -Russia: ~37,653 km -China: ~14,500 km -India: ~7,516 km (Note: Some sources report ~7,000 km or include islands for ~7,517 km) -Brazil: ~7,491 km -South Africa: ~2,798 km

questions.question: Arrange the following judicial events chronologically: 1.Berubari Union Case 2.Kesavananda Bharati Case 3.S.R. Bommai Case
questions.explanation: 1.Berubari Union Case (1960): This landmark case clarified that the Parliament can transfer Indian territory to a foreign power only by amending the Constitution under Article 368. It famously stated that the Preamble is not a part of the Constitution. 2.Kesavananda Bharati Case (1973): Delivered by the largest-ever bench of 13 judges, this case introduced the "Basic Structure Doctrine," ruling that while Parliament has wide powers to amend the Constitution, it cannot alter its basic features. It also overturned the Berubari ruling by declaring that the Preamble is an integral part of the Constitution. 3.S.R. Bommai Case (1994): This judgment addressed the misuse of Article 356 (President's Rule). It established that the power to dismiss a state government is not absolute and is subject to judicial review, while also affirming secularism and federalism as part of the basic structure.

questions.question: Which university secured 1st position in the 4th Khelo India University Games 2024 medal tally?
questions.explanation: Chandigarh University (CU) secured the 1st position in the 4th Khelo India University Games 2024 (KIUG) medal tally, finishing with a total of 71 medals, including 32 gold, 18 silver, and 21 bronzes. -Champion: Chandigarh University (32 Gold) -Runner-up: Lovely Professional University (20 Gold) -Third Place: Guru Nanak Dev University (12 Gold)

questions.question: How many pairs of decorative wheels are found on the Konark Sun Temple chariot?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple in Odisha, designed as a massive chariot for the Sun God (Surya), features 12 pairs of intricately carved stone wheels, totaling 24 wheels. These 12 pairs of wheels, situated on the base of the temple, symbolize the 12 months of the year, with each wheel representing a month.

questions.question: In which of the following movements did Mahatma Gandhi make the first use of Hunger Strike as a weapon?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi used the hunger strike as a political weapon for the first time during the Ahmedabad Mill Strike of 1918. The strike was a dispute between the cotton mill owners and workers in Ahmedabad over the discontinuation of a "plague bonus".

questions.question: ‘Veeragase’ is a traditional folk dance of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Veeragase is a traditional, high-energy folk dance primarily from the Indian state of Karnataka. It is a vigorous dance form based on Hindu mythology, typically performed by practitioners known as Lingayats or Jangama during festivals, particularly in the Shravana and Karthika months.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of Mission Mausam? 1.To enhance India’s capability in weather forecasting 2.To develop high-resolution models for improved accuracy in predicting monsoon behavior
questions.explanation: Mission Mausam is a multi-faceted central sector scheme approved in September 2024 to make India "Weather-ready and Climate-smart". Its primary objectives include: -Enhancing Forecasting Capability: Improving accuracy across all timescales, from short-range (nowcasting) to long-term seasonal forecasts. -High-Resolution Modelling: Developing advanced Earth system models and high-resolution systems (targeting a nearly 6 km spatial resolution) to better capture monsoon behaviour and extreme weather. -Infrastructure Upgrades: Deploying next-generation radars, satellites, and high-performance computing (HPC) systems. -Sectoral Support: Providing actionable advisories for agriculture, disaster management, aviation, and energy sectors to mitigate climate impacts

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of Kashmiri music and its description is correctly matched?
options: Chakri- Classical form using Santoor and Hafiz Nagma
Sufiana Music- Folk style sung with Garaha and Rabab
Hafiz Nagma- Dance based on musical notes of sufiana music
Rouf- Instrumental duet performed with Santoor and Sitar
questions.explanation: Hafiz Nagma is a traditional dance form of Kashmir where a female dancer (known as a Hafiza) performs to the accompaniment of Sufiana Kalam (classical Sufi music). The dance movements are intricately derived from the specific musical notes and rhythms of the Sufiana repertoire, representing a fusion of spiritual poetry and visual art Chakri- Classical form using Santoor and Hafiz Nagma: This is incorrect because Chakri is actually a popular folk music style, not classical. It is typically performed with instruments like the Rabab, Harmonium, and Sarangi for storytelling, rather than being centered on the Santoor. Sufiana Music- Folk style sung with Garaha and Rabab: This is incorrect because Sufiana Music (or Sufiana Kalam) is the classical music tradition of Kashmir, influenced by Persian and Central Asian styles. It primarily uses the Santoor (a 100-stringed hammered dulcimer), Sitar, and Tabla, rather than being a folk style using the Garaha. Rouf- Instrumental duet performed with Santoor and Sitar: This is incorrect because Rouf is a traditional folk dance performed primarily by women during festivals like Eid. It involves synchronized footwork and singing in a call-and-response format, not an instrumental duet.

questions.question: Consider the following sportspersons awarded the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award 2024: Sportspersons Sports 1. Gukesh D Chess 2. Harmanpreet Singh Hockey 3. Praveen Kumar Athletics 4. Manu Bhaker Shooting Which of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports announced these four recipients for India's highest sporting honour for the year 2024. They received their awards from the President of India on January 17, 2025. -Gukesh D – Chess: Recognized for his historic achievement as the youngest challenger for the World Chess Championship and his performance in the Candidates Tournament. -Harmanpreet Singh – Hockey: The captain of the Indian men's hockey team, honored for leading the team to a bronze medal at the Paris 2024 Olympics and gold at the Asian Games. -Praveen Kumar – Athletics (Para-Athletics): A para-high jumper who won the gold medal at the Paris 2024 Paralympics. -Manu Bhaker – Shooting: Honored for her historic performance at the Paris 2024 Olympics, where she became the first Indian athlete in independent India to win two medals in a single Olympic edition

questions.question: Which of the following is correct regarding sale of government assets like shares in Public sector unit?
options: It does not reduce the total assets of the government
Amount comes from sale of shares are part of revenue receipts
It creates liability for the government
Amount comes from sale of shares are part of capital receipts
questions.explanation: In government accounting, receipts are classified based on their impact on assets and liabilities. Selling shares in a Public Sector Unit (PSU), commonly known as disinvestment, is categorized as a capital receipt because it leads to a reduction in the financial assets of the government. Capital receipts are defined by two main criteria: Why other options are incorrect: It does not reduce the total assets of the government: This is incorrect because the government’s ownership stake in a PSU is a financial asset. Selling these shares directly reduces the government's total pool of financial assets. Amount comes from sale of shares are part of revenue receipts: This is incorrect because revenue receipts are recurring incomes (like taxes, interest, or dividends) that neither create a liability nor reduce assets. It creates liability for the government: This is incorrect because selling an asset is a "non-debt" transaction. It provides immediate funds without the obligation to repay them in the future. In contrast, borrowing money is what creates a liability.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding India’s Ramsar Sites: 1.India has the highest number of Ramsar sites in South Asia. 2.Keibul Lamjao is the only floating National Park in the world and is also a Ramsar site. 3.All Ramsar sites in India are located in coastal regions. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 and 2 are correct. India has the highest number of Ramsar sites in South Asia (98 as of early 2026), and Keibul Lamjao National Park in Manipur is the world's only floating national park and a designated site. However, statement 3 is incorrect because India’s Ramsar sites include many inland wetlands, not just coastal ones. -Statement 1 is Correct: India has the largest network of Ramsar sites in South Asia, with the tally reaching 98 by February 2026. -Statement 2 is Correct: Keibul Lamjao National Park is the only floating National Park in the world, located on Loktak Lake in Manipur. -Statement 3 is Incorrect: Many Ramsar sites in India are inland, such as Wular Lake in Jammu & Kashmir and Bhoj Wetland in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: Matched the dynasties listed with their respective founders. Dynasty Founder A) Tomaras 1)Sher Shah B) Mamluk 2)Khizr Khan C)Sur 3)qutb-ud-din Aibak D) Sayyid 4)Anangpal
questions.explanation: Dynasty Founder A) Tomaras 1) Anangpal B) Mamluk 2) Qutb-ud-din Aibak C)Sur 3) Sher Shah D) Sayyid 4) Khizr Khan

questions.explanation: Let three equal distance be ‘d’

questions.question: What will be the value of K for which the system of equations 5x+6y+3=0 and kx+3y+8=0 has no solution
questions.explanation: If there are two equations a1x+b1y+c1=0 & a2x+b2y+c2=0. for no solution, a1/a2 =b1/b2 ⇒5/K=6/3⇒K=2.5

questions.question: In a circle with center O and radius 13 cm, two parallel chords PQ and RS are drawn on opposite side of the center. If PQ=24cm and RS=10cm. Find the distance between the two chords.
questions.explanation: In △PAO: PA² + OA² = OP² (12)² + OA² = (13)² OA = 5 In △ROB: OB² + BR² = OR² OB² + (5)² = (13)² OB = 12 Distance b/w the chords = OA + OB = 5 + 12 = 17cm

questions.question: The right circular cone has a height of 20 cm. It is sliced by a plane parallel to the base at a distance of 5 cm from the top. What percentage of the total volume is the small cone formed at the top?
questions.explanation: Since the cones are similar, the ratio of the radii is the same as the ratio of the heights. Hence, volume of cone is proportional to the cube of height.

questions.question: In a 400 m race, if X gives Y a 40 m head start, X wins the race by 20 m. If Y gets a 100 m headstart in a 300 m race, then who would win the race and by how many metres?
questions.explanation: First Race X runs for 400 m while Y runs for only 340 m as he gets head start of 40 m and got defeated by 20 m Sx/Sy =400/340=20/17 Second Race X needs to run 300m avel Y needs to run 200 m as he gets head start of 100 m. Distance travelled by X when Y finishes 200 m = 200×20/17=4000/17=235.29m Hence, Y wins the race by (300 - 235.29)m = 64.71m

questions.question: The profit earned after selling an article for ₹ 940 is exactly same as the loss incurred after selling the same article for ₹ 710. What is the cost price of the article?
questions.explanation: Let profit earned = loss be x. then, lets equate the cost price. 940- x =x+710 ⇒2x=230⇒x=115 CP = 710+x=710+115=825

questions.question: The area of rhombus (in cm2) having each equal side to 41 cm and one of its diagonals equal to 18 cm is?
questions.explanation: In a rhombus, diagonals intersect at right angles. In △AOB: AB = 41, OB = 9 AO² + OB² = AB² AO² = 41² - 9² = 1681 - 81 = 1600 AO = 40 cm AC = 2 × AO = 80 cm Area of rhombus = (1/2) × d1 × d2 = (1/2) × 80 × 18 = 720 cm²

questions.question: A dishonest dealer marks up his goods by 20% above the cost price but uses a weight of 800 gm instead of 1 kg. Find his total profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Let us assume cost price of 1 kg be ₹1000 Marked price =₹1200 for 1 kg but he uses false weight, so he was selling only 800 gm of product So, original CP of 800gm=₹800 and he is selling for ₹1200 Hence profit %=400/800×100=50%

questions.question: Find the mean deviation about the mean for the data 3,10,10,4,7,10,5
questions.explanation: Mean = 49/7 = 7 Mean deviation = Σ(x - x̄)/n = 18/7 ≈ 2.57

questions.question: Pipe P, Q and R working together can fill a tank in 10 hours. Pipe P and Q together can fill the same tank in 15 hours, while Pipe Q and R together can fill it in 20 hours. In how many hours can Pipe Q alone fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Efficiency of R is 2 units/hr then efficiency of Q is 1 unit/hr Hence, time required by Q to fill tank =60/1=60 hours

questions.question: A loan of ₹ 10,200 is to be paid back in two equal annual instalments at 4% p.a. compound interest. Find the value of each installment.
questions.explanation: Rate= 4% =1/25 1st year = (25→26) × 26 ⇒ 650 → 676 2nd year = 625→676 Total principal =650 + 625 =1275(8 times) →10,200 Hence, each installment = 676 × 8=5408

questions.question: Two trains start from stations P and Q at the same time, travelling towards each other. They meet after 5 hours. The train from station P travels 25% faster than the train from station Q. If the total distance between station P and Q is 900 Km, find the speed of faster train.
questions.explanation: Let speed of slower train be xkm/hr and speed of faster train be 1.25x. They both collectively travelled 900 km in 5 hours. that means, Relative speed is 900/5=180km/h. Both the trains are moving in opposite direction. Hence Relative speed will be sum of both trains So, x+1.25x=180 2.25x=180 x = 80 Speed of faster train =1.25x=100km/hr.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of common tangent that can be drawn between two circles?
questions.explanation: Maximum 4 tangents can be drawn. Two direct common tangents and two transverse common tangents.

questions.question: There is a group of 6 engineers and 5 scientists, a team of four persons is to be selected to lead a project. The team must include at least two engineers. In how many ways can this team be formed?
questions.explanation: There are three ways to select the team. 2E + 2S 6C2×5C2=15×10=150 3E + 1S 6C3×5C1=20×5=100 4E + 0S 6C4×5C0=15×1=15 Total: 150 + 100 + 15 = 265

questions.question: If (x+y):(x-y)=3:1 then find the value of (x2 +y2 ):(x2 - y2).
A hot air balloon is tethered to the ground by a cable that is 200 m long. If the cable is pulled taut and makes an angle of 600 with the horizontal ground, what is the height of the hot air balloon?
questions.explanation: sin 60° = Height / 200 m √3/2 = Height / 200 Height = 100√3 m

questions.question: A sum of money at simple interest amounts to ₹815 in 3 years and to ₹854 in 4 years. Find the sum.
questions.explanation: Interest for one year =854 - 815= ₹39 So, interest for three years = 39×3=117 Hence Amont after 3 years =815 Principal + Interest for 3 yrs = 815 ⇒ Principal =815-117=₹698

questions.question: If A,B and C are three non-zero integers such that the H.C.F. of A and B is A and H.C.F. of B and C is B. What is the H.C.F. of A,B and C?
questions.explanation: HCF of A and B is A that means, A is factor of B. Similarly. HCF of B and C is B that means, B is factor of C. Concluding both statement it can be said that A is smallest common factor, that divides all three.

questions.question: A container initially holds a mixture of wine and water in the ratio 5∶3. Some quantity of this mixture is removed and replaced with an equal quantity of water. After this operation the new ratio of wine and water becomes 1:1. What percentage of original mixture was removed and replaced?
questions.explanation: Wine : Water Initial = 5 : 3 Final = 1 : 1 Let us assume initially mixture is of 80l. then, wine : water 50l:30l and in final mixture Wine: Water 40l:40l that means 10l of wine lost from 50l. Required %=10/50×100=20%

questions.question: If cosA+sinA=7/5 then find the value of tanA.
questions.explanation: cos A + sin A = 7/5 cos²A + sin²A + 2 sin A cos A = 49/25 ⇒ sin A cos A = 12/25 Now (sin²A + cos²A)/(sin A cos A) = 1/(12/25) = 25/12 tan A + cot A = 25/12 ⇒ tan A + 1/(tan A) = 25/12 by solving quadratic eqn, we will get tan A=3/4 or 4/3

questions.question: How many times do the hour hand and the minute hand of a clock form a right angle in half a day?
questions.explanation: In a 12 hour period it is only 22 times. It happens twice in between an hour, like between 12 and 1, it happens 2 times but there are two exception between 2 and 4 and between 8 and 10, it happens only once.

questions.question: Directions: There are 11 members in a family. Anisha and Anish are sister and brother respectively, whose father's sister's father is Ashish, who is the husband of Anamika. Ajay and Akash are brothers of Arzoo and Anju is the wife of Akash, whose son is Anish. Aman is the brother of the grandmother of Anisha. Aisha is the mother of Anjali, whose father is Aman. How is Anju related to Anamika?
questions.explanation: Daughter in law.

Read the following statement carefully and identify the conclusion that follows. Statement: Some computers are mobiles. No mobile is a radio. Conclusions: I. Some computers are radios. II. No computer is a radio.

In each of the following questions, a statement is given by two statements. Read the statements carefully and answer the question which follows. Statements: 1. All staff members who attended the seminar found it beneficial. 2. Ms. Chen is a staff member. Conclusions: (i) Ms. Chen found the seminar beneficial. (ii) All staff members found the seminar beneficial.
questions.explanation: Conclusion (i) does not follow because statement (i) applies only to staff who attended the seminar, but there is no information confirming that Ms. Chen attended it—despite being a staff member as per statement (ii). Conclusion (ii) does not follow because it assumes all staff attended and found it beneficial, but statement (i) limits the benefit to only those who attended, excluding non-attendees.

questions.question: If 18 October 2006 was Wednesday, then what was the day of the week on 17 October 2000?
questions.explanation: 2000-2006 = 6+1 = 7 odd days That is 18 October 2000 = Wednesday (Same as given because 7 odd days) Then, 17 October 2000 will be Tuesday.

questions.question: You observe a colleague accessing social media sites extensively on the company computer for non-work purposes during office time. What is the best approach?
options: Confront the colleague immediately and tell them to stop.
Ignore the matter, as it's their personal choice.
Politely remind them of the internet usage policy first.
Inform your supervisor or IT department about the policy violation.
questions.explanation: Inform your supervisor or IT department about the policy violation. Excessive personal internet use reduces productivity and risks company data security. A polite reminder is good initially if minor, but repeated or extensive use requires formal reporting to enforce policies objectively.

Statement: The airline has cancelled all flights to the northern region due to severe weather conditions. Conclusions: I. Severe weather is affecting the northern region. II. The airline will resume flights once the weather improves.
questions.explanation: Only I follows the cancellation explicitly states severe weather as the reason, so I logically follows from the cause. II assumes future action without confirmation—the airline might cancel for other reasons later.

questions.explanation: Immediate right of B.

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following set. NOTE: Operations should be performed in the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. Eg. 13-Operations on 13 such as adding/ subtracting/ multiplying etc. ta 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed. [ 4 5 320] [ 6 4 864]

A number has been denoted to each of the given letters. Select the option from the following four possible arrangements of these numbers that forms a meaningful word. 1=P, 2=R, 3=U, 4=C, 5=T, 6=A, 7=E
